title: Sandhi Usage Event (v1)
description: The neutral, cross-repo boundary object emitted once per model call. Carries measured units only — NO dollars, NO tier/SKU names. Measurement lives here; pricing is a downstream commercial concern (AnvaiOps ADR-0047 D3). Superset of the AnvaiOps `llm_tokens` event and the ADR-0024 `anvai-plane-core` metering envelope.

schema_version.description: Wire-contract major version. Breaking changes bump this and coordinate across all consumers.

request_id.description: Unique id for the single model call this event meters.

occurred_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the call completed (usage finalized).

provider.description: Provider slug, neutral (e.g. anthropic, openai, gemini, bedrock, cohere, vllm, ollama, byo). Never a commercial tier/SKU name.

model.description: Model identifier as sent to the provider.

backend.description: external = provider API (billed in tokens); self_hosted = local vLLM/TGI (billed in GPU-hours downstream — token counts are for quota/usage display, not the cost basis).

virtual_key_id.description: The Sandhi virtual key that made the call (one shared upstream key fronts many virtual keys). Null for direct/unkeyed in-process use.

subject_id.description: The user the call is attributed to. Opaque, operator-assigned.

group_id.description: The team/group the call is attributed to. Opaque, operator-assigned; also the default prompt-cache namespace (ADR-0047 D9).

route.description: Optional caller-supplied route/label (e.g. the calling feature or endpoint).

session_id.description: Conversation/session key used for prompt-cache + KV affinity (ADR-0047 D9). Preserved end-to-end so hosted prompt caches keep hitting and self-hosted KV routing stays sticky. Never collapsed to a single value.

tokens_out.description: Completion tokens, finalized from the terminal usage block (never estimated mid-stream).

cache_creation_tokens.description: Prompt-cache write tokens (priced ~1.25x fresh input for Anthropic). Attributed per subject/session.

cache_read_tokens.description: Prompt-cache read tokens (priced ~0.1x fresh input). If A warms a shared prefix and B reads it, B's event shows the cheap read.

usage_completeness.description: Whether token counts are final, partial, or unavailable for this logical call.

attempts.description: Number of upstream attempts made for this logical call.

outcome.description: Stable terminal outcome such as success, error, or cancelled.

upstream_request_id.description: Provider-supplied request identifier when one was returned.

gpu_seconds.description: Self-hosted backends only: GPU-seconds of compute. The cost basis for self_hosted (token counts are display-only there).
